Title: To Poison a King (Heirs of Isla, #1)
Author: S.G. Prince
My Review:
i havent read a book this fervently in so long, it was so easy to be drawn into the plot and great narration. and the main characters were written very well, especially selene. i loved reading through her voice and adored her as a main character.

this wouldve easily been a 4 or even 4.5 star if it weren’t for Two things…. One, the weird asf age gap. although the romance is just a subplot, it’s still written about significantly so this was hard to ignore. i get why she had to be young at the start of the story but i dont get why he had to be so much older. completely unnecessary, and it made me so angry bc i loved the two characters and their dynamic but i couldnt root for them knowing he knew her since she was FOURTEEN…. i had to fully delude and gaslight myself into thinking he wasn’t that much older so my reading experience could be better, but regardless for that reason i deduct a star. Also i have a small bone to pick with the ending…. it felt kinda rushed considering how big of a moment it was. The decision was made in a second and then they just moved on and then the book ended in another two pages. it felt a little sloppy and could’ve been written better and deeper considering the fact that the most of the book up until the very end, especially the first half, was written really well.

Anyway, overall great read if u have the ability to gaslight yourself into believing the characters are the ages you choose

Title: Earthlings
Author: Sayaka Murata
My Review:
this was so disturbing, i actually dont know what ive just experienced. it was the same feeling i got from 'things have gotten worse since we last spoke' but this was better i think (??)

but there were good discussions of social alienation and implications of childhood trauma. it was a rlly good development from her childhood into her adulthood and how her abuse resulted in this delirious escapism.

i knew the content warnings when i started reading the books but because of muratas descriptions and narration i felt sick sometimes lmao. especially in the beginning when she was a child. her obvious naivety in her narration was so depressing and disturbing to read in the given circumstances like her sexual assault and the . cannibalism

i dont know what ive taken away But i did take something away from this book. but honestly reading this just felt like a fever dream. cant even rate it.

Title: Beach Read
Author: Emily Henry
My Review:
this was a lot better than i expected. first of all, gus was everything and honestly i liked how he, january, and their relationship was written and evolved. they were so messy but that made them enjoyable. also the cult stuff and research was so fun to read. the pacing was weird though and i dont really think i felt attached to anyone except gus. and although i enjoyed the cult stuff, the bet had sm more potential and i feel like emily henry only grazed it.

this might be biased bc i bought the book and i refuse to think that i spent money on a book rated less than 3 stars and besides nusaybah set my expectations for this book quite low. but i do think i genuinely enjoyed this book.
